title: TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES - 211info
text: LIFEWORKS NW KING TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual/group skills training, supported employment/education, supported housing, life skills, and psychiatric treatment for transition-age youth with severe mental illnesses impacting their ability to successfully function in the community and achieve normal developmental milestones. The STRIDE program connects transition-age youth with services that support well-being and independence, including peer support, case management, outreach, and engagement. 3716 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Transitional Age Youth services: Ages 16-24 with severe mental illnesses STRIDE program: Ages 14-25 with mental illness Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 619-9007 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Program phone (503) 619-9007 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake FEES: Varies by program. Nearly all LifeWorks Northwest programs accept OHP/Medicaid and Medicare. Some programs serve uninsured individuals or offer free or discounted services. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable AREA SERVED: Multnomah County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 8am-8pm, Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet Bus 6 Services: Independent Living Skills Instruction * Mental Health Issues Psychiatric Case Management Psychiatric Case Management * Adolescents Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - INTENSIVE COMMUNITY-BASED SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PARENT EDUCATION AND SUPPORT MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH CARE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - SEVERE MENTAL ILLNESS SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ADULT SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - YOUTH SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
